title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, 19, closed his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney suggests James Hagens could sign an entry-level contract soon as BC's season ends.
James Hagens, the 2025 NHL Draft's seventh pick, could join Bruins, providing a boost for the team's playoff push.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Bradley Cooper directs Will Arnett in 'Is This Thing On?', a comedy-drama streaming on Disney+.
Chuck Norris's 'Lone Wolf McQuade' is streaming on Tubi, Pluto TV, Plex, Prime Video, and YouTube.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' featuring Cillian Murphy is now available on Netflix.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora enters his eighth season as Red Sox manager, focusing on the present despite previous losing seasons.
Cora's contract with the Red Sox runs through 2027, with talks on his future expected to begin in 2026.
Cora is 134 wins away from surpassing Terry Francona for second-most managerial wins with the Red Sox.

title: The Bruins’ second line isn’t just a pleasant surprise. They’ve been one of the NHL’s best this year.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins' second line, featuring Zacha, Arvidsson, and Mittelstadt, boasts a goal differential of +19 this season.
With 472 minutes of 5-on-5 play, the line has outscored opponents 35-16, showing consistent offensive strength.
Arvidsson, Zacha, and Mittelstadt have become key contributors, pushing Boston toward a potential playoff spot.

title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Andrew Mahr, 37, from Needham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support MGH pediatric cancer care.
Mahr's motivation stems from experiences with young MGH patients and witnessing the 2013 Boston Marathon bombing.
This is Mahr's second time running for MGH, aiming to raise awareness and funds for pediatric cancer research.

title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Proposal to cut Massachusetts income tax from 5% to 4% could appear on state ballot in November 2026.
The tax cut is estimated to reduce state revenue by $5.1 billion annually, saving median households about $1,250 a year.
Governor Maura Healey opposes the cut, citing potential harm to state finances, while 78% of surveyed readers support it.

title: A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church is on trial for allegedly murdering her boyfriend, Leroy Fowler, with antifreeze due to his affair.
Prosecutors claim Church had previously joked about poisoning Fowler, and Fowler's family suspected poisoning before his death.
The defense argues Church was venting her emotions and filmed Fowler to show him the effects of substance abuse.

title: Healey pushes to get rid of Sunday hunting ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey is proposing legislation to end Massachusetts' Sunday hunting ban and allow crossbow use for all hunters.
MassWildlife supports reducing bowhunting setback distances and lifting the Sunday ban to manage deer overpopulation.
The legislation aims to align Massachusetts with other states and improve conservation and food security efforts.

title: Cape Cod magnet fisher pulls ‘pristine-looking’ derringer pistol from Bridgewater river
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Magnet fisher Nate DeMontigny found a derringer pistol in a 'brand new' case from a Bridgewater river.
Upon discovering the pistol, DeMontigny contacted police who retrieved and documented the firearm.
The discovery, posted online, quickly went viral, gaining 1.2 million views on a magnet fishing subreddit.

title: Teen charged with murder after stabbing in Chelsea
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: 18-year-old Sergio Josue Castellanos faces murder charge after fatal stabbing in Chelsea Thursday night.
Chelsea police found multiple stabbing victims near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue; exact number not released.
25-year-old victim died after surgery; investigation ongoing, police urge information at 617-466-4800.
